danger_signals.visual_form: Hordes of undead and demonic creatures throughout stages
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Arthur loses armor with a single hit, left in boxers
danger_signals.meaning: Extreme vulnerability state; one additional hit results in death

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character as final stage threat
Princess Prin-Prin captured by demon forces
danger_signals.meaning: World-state of peril requiring player intervention

safety_signals.meaning: Contains better armor and weapons for improved protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Improved armor variants found in chests

safety_signals.meaning: Offensive capability improvement for threat mitigation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hordes of ghosts, goblins, and demonic entities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demon-infested stages filled with undead enemies and dark environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse hostile territory defeating enemies to reach Astaroth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite shows Arthur in boxers after armor loss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extreme caution required; single hit causes failure; enemy patterns become more threatening

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage completion visuals trigger difficulty escalation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to replay stages at higher difficulty for 'true' ending; increased enemy density and aggression

environmental_storytelling.example: Demon World Village (Makaimura) versus rescue mission context establishes good vs. evil geographical divide
Princess Prin-Prin's kidnapped status communicated through her absence and placement as final stage goal
Hidden treasure chests signal that survival requires preparation and discovery; armor/weapons must be earned
Progression through hordes of undead indicates escalating demonic control of territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can withstand one hit; capable of combat and progression
Vulnerable; requires extreme caution and single-hit avoidance; movement becomes defensive
Constant threat patrolling and attacking; player must navigate or eliminate
Boss-level threat requiring skill and preparation; represents world-state conflict apex

notes: Ghosts 'n Goblins uses a binary visual communication system: armored Arthur (safe/capable) vs. unarmored Arthur (critical danger). The game world manifests as a demonic occupation requiring invasion and rescue. Difficulty escalation upon completion is a meta-manifestation of story necessity (true ending requires additional proof). The series is known for punishing difficulty, which is visually reinforced through enemy density and one-hit armor loss mechanic. Faction identity is primarily narrative (good knight vs. demon king) rather than color-coded or symbol-based.
